concerns.text: The plan relies on 'Local file / Synthetic (No verified URL)' for the GTFS Feed. While the spec allows synthetic data if no verified URL exists, the plan fails to cite a specific, reproducible source for the 'standard public GTFS feed' (e.g., NYC MTA) or the synthetic generation method. Relying on a generic 'download a public feed' instruction violates the Constitution Principle I (Reproducibility) which mandates fetching from the 'same canonical source on every run'. The plan must specify the exact dataset source (URL or DOI) or the deterministic synthetic generation parameters to ensure the experiment is reproducible.

The plan assumes a dataset size of '~10k text sequences' derived from a 'Single city GTFS feed'. This volume is likely insufficient to train a 1.5B parameter model to learn complex topological rules from scratch (SFT) without severe overfitting, especially given the 'held-out' requirement for generalization. The plan does not justify this sample size against the model capacity or cite a source for the feasibility of learning topology at this scale. This risks a methodology failure where the model fails to learn the task not due to the 'map-free' constraint, but due to data starvation.
